column1: Inland Communities | TD 4's current track should keep the tropical storm force winds (39+ mph) to your west, which is why you have not been upgraded to a Tropical Storm Warning. A wobble east could change that so keep checking in with us.

I just got a warning from a Pinellas County stating surge is now 2-4 feet. If you add high tide for Ozona which is 2.9 ft at 12:20 Sunday, that adds up to 5-7 feet. I’m at 7 feet above see level. We’re sandbagging for sure. I had water after Idalia at high tide.
Love this. Look forward to seeing this graphic information more north(Marion county)
Marion isn't his area. He does try to show quite a bit, but you may want to try Eric Burris for Central FL.
thanks
thank you. Didn't realize.
How much rain is expected? Trying to figure out how much to drain my pool.
https://www.nhc.noaa.gov/.../graphics_at4.../094934.shtml...
You know what that say, a wobble West is the best!
As a retired broadcast journalist (ABC radio affiliate) from California, I say you do an excellent job in reporting!
I'm in Venice and contemplating my.storm shutters. Do we have a wind guesstimate?

we r in englewood and just closed ours ..we figured just in case (better now than when it’s raining) + the house next door that was destroyed by Ian STILL hasn’t been taken down !! So im sure all summer we will deal with the debris 🥹
same. We got crushed in ian
I'll decide later this eve
no shutters, this is a summer storm, not a hurricane
you don't need it
Venice won't see anything at all
Good morning Denis What’s up for Homosassa area, lol moved from Tampa and this is first time in this area 🙏🏻
There may be flooding in Citrus County. He's been saying that since yesterday. The farther west it goes, the better.
thanks Megan, we will be prepared as always just in case. I was told when we were looking to buy a home to always stay on the other right side of 19 for those reasons

I am in Citrus County...well in CT and supposed to fly Home tomorrow late night..not sure if that will happen...anyways check your flood zone bc flooding in those zone is typically bad. If there is an evacuation please follow them.
I am supposed to visit Crystal River tomorrow - Weds. Been wondering what to do bc of flooding.. sigh.
check the evacuation zone of where you are visiting. If it is a flood zone I would wait, so many have to be rescued bc of the water..either stuck in home or vehicle.
good luck 🙏
rain and some gusty winds.You will be ok

I know it's not as strong as Hurricane Idalia last August, but that track is very similar to TD4's and caused a lot of storm surge flooding for us low lying folks in Citrus County. Prepping for the worst, hoping for the best. Thank you for all you do, Denis Phillips!

Yeah, everyone glad for the West shift isn't paying attention. That leaves it out in the Gulf longer, where we don't want it. Hitting land earlier is better. Hope you all don't get too much water! I have dear friends in Crystal River that have flooded before too.
crystal river always gets flooded badly..prayers for your friend...hope all will be well
This is my concern. Just a few hrs before passing TB, it just kept strengthening in the gulf. The storm surge prediction was so scary. It went from 1-3" to 4-7", in a day.
Yes it caused a lot of problems for low lying folks in CR. Fortunately we haven’t flooded, but people do a couple blocks from us..
he’s reported
weirdo

So my flight for a very important reason to travel is probably canceled?????

my friend was supposed to fly in Sunday afternoon and they canceled her flight already. They put her on a 7am Sunday incoming flight instead. If you are leaving at 8am maybe you’ll be one of the last flights out? Good luck!
I’m going out to help my grand parents, this isn’t a “vacation”, gram has Parkinson’s and dementia. Was going to help my papa, giving him a break etc💔

I’m gonna say there’s a pretty good chance your flight is going to be delayed love. 😞. It also depends on where you’re flying to. Are you flying back home? Just get ready like you normally would and just keep an eye out for cancellations.
well the storm is coming through later tonight and all day tomorrow. If your flight isn’t until this upcoming next Friday the 9th then you are all clear. The storm will be gone.
it’s all good. Everything should be back to normal in a few days

Going west means it stays out in the Gulf and can strengthen, causing more damage when it hits land. Hitting land earlier and riding the coast as was actually a safer scenario for other areas.
